title: Fly me to the moon
wikitext:
[]: Fly me to the moon!             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 15, 2004 11:14 pm I've always wondered why this particular piece was used as Evangelion's ending song, and why it fits so well. It is rather unusual to find english songs composed outside of Japan in anime. Anyone have any idea to whose decision it was to include this song and why?

[]:             Quote Post by Cyanna » Wed Sep 15, 2004 11:34 pm Maybe it's irony. It sounds like your favorite dream...which is an odd way to end Shinji's worst nightmares...

[]: Re: Fly me to the moon!             Quote Post by advent_child » Thu Sep 16, 2004 4:48 am Dave wrote: I've always wondered why this particular piece was used as Evangelion's ending song, and why it fits so well. It is rather unusual to find english songs composed outside of Japan in anime. Anyone have any idea to whose decision it was to include this song and why?

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Thu Sep 16, 2004 4:06 pm I know you would think it would be on Galaxy Express 999 or Gundam or somthing!

[]:             Quote Post by belial11737 » Thu Sep 16, 2004 4:35 pm Cyanna wrote: Maybe it's irony. It sounds like your favorite dream...which is an odd way to end Shinji's worst nightmares...

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Thu Sep 16, 2004 6:26 pm Ah, Fly me to dah moon.... I like the song. What I don't like is the many different versions of it shown at the end of Eva. It just seemed like they picked as many musical styles as possible to use with one song. While not a bad idea, I don't think it worked out all that well in the end. Some of them kinda make my ears bleed. Bloody ears = t3h music4l sux0rz. I'm just glad no one in the audio/music department working on Eva at the time thought of using rap :shock: That probably wouldn't end well.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Sep 16, 2004 8:05 pm it would be interesting though

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Fri Sep 17, 2004 3:42 pm oh god a rap if that happend id chop off my own ears and eat'em! I really dont like rap i hate rap well emeime or what ever is kool the white rapper the veterans of this site know i cant spell for crap so yeah

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Sep 18, 2004 4:54 pm I think the reason they chose "Fly Me To The Moon" was probably because it was known early on in the series that the moon (or moons) would play a significant role in the story, whether literally or symbolically. As it unfolded, it became more symbolic than was probably intended (save for the large presence of the Black Moon, and the somewhat more subdued role of Luna). There was probably a vague idea of how everything would turn out, and the reference would have been more obvious. Also, the "I Love You" line, well, that's obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by Gilgamesh » Sat Sep 18, 2004 8:33 pm I'm actually surprised to be hearing all of this. Personally I hated "Fly Me to the Moon." Both me and my friend who introduced me to Evangelion agreed that it was one of the worst songs ever. And the horrible remixes-ugh. God! No more! FLY ME TO THE MOON...TROPICAL REMIX....FLY ME TO THE MOON...LUNAR REMIX....FLY ME TO THE MOON...UNDERWATER THEME REMIX....FLY ME TO THE MOON...CRAPPY DEATH HEAVY METAL GOTHY NONSENSE REMIX...FLY ME TO THE MOON...WEIRD COMPUTER "BLEEP" AND "BLOOP" SOUNDS REMIX...FLY ME TO THE MOON...RAPPER REMIX...FLY ME TO THE MOON...MESOAMERICAN RITUAL CHANT REMIX... AAAAAAAH STOP IT STOP IT STOP IT STOP IT!!!!!!!!!! IT WAS BAD THE FIRST TIME, WORSE THE SECOND, EVEN WORSE IN THE THIRD, AREN'T YOU SATISFIED WITH THE MONSTER YOU HAVE CREATED?! Zankoku Na Tenshi No TEEZE however is excellent.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Sat Sep 18, 2004 8:51 pm screw you, i liked fly me to the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Gilgamesh » Sat Sep 18, 2004 9:11 pm ShoKenju wrote: screw you, i liked fly me to the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Sat Sep 18, 2004 9:29 pm Gilgamesh wrote: ShoKenju wrote: screw you, i liked fly me to the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sat Sep 18, 2004 9:43 pm Gilgamesh wrote: ShoKenju wrote: screw you, i liked fly me to the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sun Sep 19, 2004 5:13 am You don't HAVE to listen to all those versions if you don't like them Gilgamesh. Leave them to the rest of us who like to sing along!

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Sun Sep 19, 2004 8:35 am You don't HAVE to listen to all those versions if you don't like them Gilgamesh. Leave them to the rest of us who like to sing along!

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Sun Sep 19, 2004 9:28 am Timesplitter 01 wrote: Gilgamesh wrote: ShoKenju wrote: screw you, i liked fly me to the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Sep 19, 2004 3:29 pm Mommy! Daddy! Stop fighting; you''re ruining my birthday! Fly Me To The Moon is the best song ever. Frai Mi Tu Da Muun was just as good. All y'all need to shut up, or I'll fly YOU to the moon! BTW, Kiel, it was Addition.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sun Sep 19, 2004 3:49 pm Kiel wrote: Dave, have you ever heard the episode 27 audio track from...one of the Japanese soundtracks? (I don't know which ).

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Sun Sep 19, 2004 4:06 pm in the series i didn't like it at all but the version that i got off the internet rocked
summary:
[]: Fly me to the moon!             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 15, 2004 11:14 pm I've always wondered why this particular piece was used as Evangelion's ending song, and why it fits so well. It is rather unusual to find english songs composed outside of Japan in anime. Anyone have any idea to whose decision it was to include this song and why?

[]:             Quote Post by Cyanna
